displayTitle: Aim the Sound, Then Prove the Setup
kicker: UT LEVEL I // ANGLE BEAM TO PROCEDURE

beats.narration: Weld flaws stand vertical, edge-on to a straight beam. So the procedure tilts the sound and sends a shear wave in at an angle.

payload.title: AIM THE SOUND
payload.sub: Angle-beam weld exams, exactly as the procedure writes them.

beats.narration: The procedure picks the wedge, not your preference. Seventy degrees reaches along thin sections, sixty covers mid-range, forty-five suits heavy plate.
The beam travels the steel in a vee. Skip equals two times thickness times tangent theta; each leg is thickness over cosine. That sets your scan band.

payload.heading: Skip and legs are trigonometry
payload.note: SKIP = 2T·TAN θ   ·   LEG = T / COS θ

beats.narration: Before the probe touches the weld, prove the tools. Find the true index point, verify the angle within two degrees, then build the DAC.
Get the angle verified and the curve drawn, and the hard part of angle-beam work is already behind you.

payload.sub: Angle, distance, DAC, band — then the weld.
